aging_system.philosophy: Aging is never a sudden cliff. Changes are gradual, announced by small signals before they become mechanical penalties. The numbers shift slowly underneath the narrative. Knowledge and social skills actively improve with age — only physical skills ceiling and decline.
aging_system.exceptional_life_note: Surviving to 90 in Arneth means something. The Archive of Keth wants to talk to them. Small communities treat them as living history.
aging_system.note_on_pace: A player aging their character from 22 to 30 lives through 5,200 in-game days — enough for the world simulation to have done significant things. The character ages into a changed world.

age_bands.label: Youth
age_bands.physical_growth: Peak growth. Skills climb fast. Body resilient. Injuries heal quickly and cleanly.
age_bands.cognitive_social: Low credibility. Dismissed by older NPCs unless demonstrably competent. Must prove themselves repeatedly.
age_bands.passive_skill_gains: Physical skills accelerate 1.2x through active use
age_bands.npc_disposition_modifier: Minor NPCs often underestimate. May be patronizing. Can be exploited or ignored.
age_bands.flavor: The body is ahead of the reputation. Everything is possible and nothing is proven.

age_bands.label: Prime
age_bands.physical_growth: Physical peak. High ceiling. Injuries heal well. Recovery fast.
age_bands.cognitive_social: Credibility building. Starting to be taken seriously without prior proof.
age_bands.passive_skill_gains: Balanced — physical and cognitive both advancing well
age_bands.npc_disposition_modifier: Neutral baseline. Treated as a peer by most.
age_bands.flavor: Best overall window. Peak physical meets growing social weight. The most capable decade.

age_bands.label: Middle Years
age_bands.physical_growth: Subtle plateau. Morning stiffness is real. Recovery slightly longer. Not yet limiting.
age_bands.cognitive_social: Solid authority. Minor NPCs defer. Taken seriously without proof in most contexts.
age_bands.passive_skill_gains: Knowledge and social skills gain a passive +1 point per month from accumulated context
age_bands.passive_skill_losses: Physical skills above 400 begin very slow decay without active maintenance
age_bands.npc_disposition_modifier: Slight positive modifier in professional and civic contexts.
age_bands.flavor: The productive middle. Experience begins compensating for physical plateau. The most successful characters in the world are usually here.

age_bands.label: Experience
age_bands.physical_growth: Noticeable physical change. Technique becomes essential. A younger opponent with equal skill wins on endurance.
age_bands.cognitive_social: High authority. Reputation precedes entry into a room.
age_bands.passive_skill_gains: Knowledge and social skills gain +2 points per month passively
age_bands.passive_skill_losses: Physical skills above 300 decay without consistent active use. Rate: 1 point per week idle.
age_bands.npc_disposition_modifier: Meaningful positive modifier. Even hostile NPCs grant grudging respect to age and evident competence.
age_bands.compensation_rule: High Tactics or deep weapon knowledge creates qualitative advantages against younger opponents — makes fights less predictable, not necessarily winnable
age_bands.flavor: Experience fights back but loses to raw youth in direct contest. The gap shows in long fights, hard travel, cold mornings.

age_bands.label: Elder
age_bands.physical_growth: Real limitation. Physical ceiling dropping perceptibly. Illness risk rising.
age_bands.cognitive_social: Deep authority. People listen. In the right contexts, a word carries more weight than a sword.
age_bands.passive_skill_gains: Knowledge and social skills gain +3 points per month passively
age_bands.passive_skill_losses: Physical skills decay 2 points per week without active use. Ceiling actively lowers 1 point per month.
age_bands.injury_recovery_modifier: Injuries take 50% longer to heal. Serious wounds have a permanent penalty chance.
age_bands.npc_disposition_modifier: Strong positive modifier in civilian and administrative contexts. Military NPCs are neutral — they see the physical limitation.
age_bands.flavor: Surviving to 70 means something in this world. NPCs notice. It opens certain doors and closes others.

age_bands.label: Old
age_bands.physical_growth: Significant limitation. Physical capacity reduced substantially.
age_bands.cognitive_social: Extraordinary social weight if earned. Unearned age is just fragility.
age_bands.passive_skill_gains: Knowledge and social only. +3 points per month.
age_bands.passive_skill_losses: Physical skills decay 3 points per week without active use. Ceiling lowers 2 points per month.
age_bands.injury_recovery_modifier: Injuries take twice as long. Serious injuries may be permanently disabling.
age_bands.npc_disposition_modifier: Significant positive in the right contexts. In others, pity or exploitation.
age_bands.flavor: Rare. Treated differently by NPCs. The world has seen most of these people die already.

age_bands.label: Ancient
age_bands.physical_growth: Very limited. Physical engagement is hazardous.
age_bands.cognitive_social: Near-legendary in small communities. Living history in larger ones.
age_bands.passive_skill_gains: Knowledge and social only. +2 points per month (declining energy).
age_bands.passive_skill_losses: Physical: 5 points per week. Ceiling: 3 points per month.
age_bands.illness_risk_modifier: +60% base disease probability. Winter is genuinely dangerous.
age_bands.npc_disposition_modifier: Unique reactions — some NPCs are reverent, some are predatory (old people with resources are targets), some are merely curious.
age_bands.special: Characters at this age may have personal memory of events from 60+ years ago — living sources of history unavailable in any book.
age_bands.flavor: Very rare. Mentioned before you arrive. People have heard of you.

age_bands.label: Legend
age_bands.cognitive_social: A living piece of history. The Archive of Keth sends people to record their recollections.
age_bands.passive_skill_losses: Physical: 8 points per week. Cognitive: slight decline begins — memory inconsistencies.
age_bands.illness_risk_modifier: +80% base disease probability. A bad winter is existential.
age_bands.npc_disposition_modifier: Extraordinary. Almost universally treated with deference or fascination.
age_bands.flavor: Exceptional. The world did not expect them to survive this long and is not quite sure what to do with them.

graceful_aging_signals.description: Before mechanical penalties kick in, the character receives narrative signals. These are flavor text events that appear before the numbers change.

examples.signal: The morning stiffness lasts a bit longer than it used to. Nothing serious.
A young opponent in a sparring match was slightly faster than expected.
You find yourself gauging a fight more carefully than you once did. The instinct to find another way is stronger.
Cold mornings are a different experience than they were. The joints remember old injuries.
A younger soldier offered you a hand on a steep section of road. You weren't sure whether to be grateful or insulted.
Someone referred to you as 'the old one' without any particular disrespect. They meant it as a compliment.

causes_of_death.notes: Primary killer of middle-aged characters. Peaks in winter and Breaking events.
Even minor injuries kill if untreated. No age preference.
Most common in 16–40 band. Older characters are less likely to be in combat.
Winter mortality in bad years. Hits elderly and very young first.
Travel in winter or The Breaking without preparation.
Significant mortality risk. Modeled in population simulation.
Miners, smiths, soldiers, sailors all have elevated mortality. Durnwald Mine collapse is an example.

knowledge_social_aging_bonus.description: Accumulated context and pattern recognition. An old merchant's clerk who hasn't touched a ledger in years still understands commerce better than a young one who has.
knowledge_social_aging_bonus.rate: Varies by age band — see age band entries for monthly passive gain rates

skill_decay_floors.rule: A skill never decays below half its peak recorded level, rounded down.
skill_decay_floors.example: A character who reached Expert (380 on 500 scale) in swordsmanship, then stopped fighting for a decade, performs at roughly Journeyman/Skilled boundary — around 190. The knowledge is there. The edge is gone. Rebuilding is faster than original development.
skill_decay_floors.below_journeyman: Skills below 150 do not decay — basic competencies persist.
skill_decay_floors.rebuilding_rate: Re-training a decayed skill returns to peak at 2x normal progression rate — the body remembers.
